questions.prompt: A user accidentally orange-clouded their mail subdomain. Within minutes, email stops working. Why?

choices.text: Cloudflare's proxy mode handles HTTP/HTTPS only — it doesn't proxy SMTP or other mail protocols. The mail server suddenly resolves to Cloudflare's edge IPs which don't accept mail. Always keep mail (MX records, mail subdomain) gray-cloud / DNS-only.

questions.explanation: Cloudflare proxies HTTP/HTTPS (and WebSocket). It doesn't proxy mail or arbitrary TCP. Anything non-HTTP must stay gray-cloud. Mail is the most common case people get wrong.

questions.prompt: Your origin is on Vercel. They only support CNAME, not A records. You want `example.com` to point at Vercel. What does Cloudflare let you do?

choices.text: Manually convert CNAME to A records.

Use CNAME on the apex with CNAME flattening. Cloudflare allows CNAME records on the root domain (which standard DNS disallows) and resolves them to A/AAAA at query time. Set `example.com CNAME cname.vercel-dns.com` and Cloudflare handles the flattening automatically.

questions.explanation: CNAME flattening is a Cloudflare feature that solves the 'can't CNAME on apex' problem for hosted services with rotating IPs. Set up CNAME on the apex; Cloudflare resolves to current A/AAAA records at query time.
